Analisis Database Sakila

Angelica Ardilla

25 Mei 2026

Analisis Database Sakila
Eksplorasi, Visualisasi & Uji Statistik Data Rental Film

1 Pendahuluan

Database Sakila adalah database contoh (sample database) yang disediakan secara resmi oleh MySQL. Database ini mensimulasikan sistem manajemen toko penyewaan film DVD dengan struktur data yang lengkap dan realistis.

Laporan ini menyajikan 9 analisis utama yang mencakup:

  • 🎬 Analisis konten dan katalog film
  • 👥 Analisis profil dan perilaku pelanggan
  • 💰 Analisis pendapatan dan transaksi
  • 👨‍💼 Analisis performa staf dan toko

2 Statistika Deskriptif

2.1 Tabel Film

Variabel numerik utama pada tabel film:

summary(film[, c("rental_duration","rental_rate","length","replacement_cost")])
##  rental_duration  rental_rate       length      replacement_cost
##  Min.   :3.000   Min.   :0.99   Min.   : 46.0   Min.   : 9.99   
##  1st Qu.:4.000   1st Qu.:0.99   1st Qu.: 80.0   1st Qu.:14.99   
##  Median :5.000   Median :2.99   Median :114.0   Median :19.99   
##  Mean   :4.985   Mean   :2.98   Mean   :115.3   Mean   :19.98   
##  3rd Qu.:6.000   3rd Qu.:4.99   3rd Qu.:149.2   3rd Qu.:24.99   
##  Max.   :7.000   Max.   :4.99   Max.   :185.0   Max.   :29.99

Distribusi rating film:

as.data.frame(table(Rating = film$rating))

2.2 Tabel Payment

cat("Statistika Deskriptif Amount:\n")
## Statistika Deskriptif Amount:
print(summary(payment$amount))
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
##   0.000   2.990   3.990   4.201   4.990  11.990
cat("Standar Deviasi  :", round(sd(payment$amount), 4))
## Standar Deviasi  : 2.363
cat("Total Transaksi  :", nrow(payment))
## Total Transaksi  : 16044
cat("Total Revenue    : $", formatC(sum(payment$amount),
    format = "f", digits = 2, big.mark = ","))
## Total Revenue    : $ 67,406.56

2.3 Tabel Customer

cat("Total Pelanggan :", nrow(customer))
## Total Pelanggan : 599
cat("Aktif           :", sum(customer$active == 1))
## Aktif           : 584
cat("Tidak Aktif     :", sum(customer$active == 0))
## Tidak Aktif     : 15
cat("% Aktif         :", round(mean(customer$active==1)*100, 2), "%\n\n")
## % Aktif         : 97.5 %
print(as.data.frame(table(Store = customer$store_id)))
##   Store Freq
## 1     1  326
## 2     2  273

2.4 Tabel Rental & Inventori

cat("Total Transaksi Rental        :", nrow(rental))
## Total Transaksi Rental        : 16044
cat("Rental belum dikembalikan     :", sum(is.na(rental$return_date)))
## Rental belum dikembalikan     : 183
cat("Total Item Inventori          :", nrow(inventory))
## Total Item Inventori          : 4581
cat("Distribusi Inventori per Store:\n")
## Distribusi Inventori per Store:
print(as.data.frame(table(Store = inventory$store_id)))
##   Store Freq
## 1     1 2270
## 2     2 2311

3 Analisis & Visualisasi

3.1 Distribusi Rating Film

1 
Distribusi Rating Film
Proporsi jumlah film berdasarkan kategori rating MPAA
ℹ️
Rating film menggunakan standar MPAA: G (semua usia), PG (bimbingan orang tua), PG-13 (usia 13+), R (dewasa), dan NC-17 (18+). 
rating_data <- dbGetQuery(con, "
  SELECT rating, COUNT(*) AS jumlah_film
  FROM film GROUP BY rating ORDER BY jumlah_film DESC")

rating_data$persen <- round(rating_data$jumlah_film /
                            sum(rating_data$jumlah_film) * 100, 2)
rating_data
ggplot(rating_data, aes(x = "", y = jumlah_film, fill = rating)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 1, color = "white", linewidth = 0.8) +
  coord_polar(theta = "y", start = 0) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("G"     = "#0f7b6c",
                               "PG"    = "#c8922a",
                               "PG-13" = "#0d1b2a",
                               "R"     = "#c0392b",
                               "NC-17" = "#7d3c98")) +
  labs(title    = "Distribusi Rating Film di Database Sakila",
       subtitle = "Proporsi jumlah film per kategori rating MPAA",
       fill = "Rating",
       caption = "Sumber: Database Sakila") +
  theme_void(base_size = 13) +
  theme(plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5,
                                     size = 14, family = "serif"),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, color = "gray50",
                                     margin = margin(b = 10)),
        plot.caption  = element_text(color = "gray60", size = 9),
        legend.position = "right")

📝 Interpretasi

Rating PG-13 mendominasi dengan 223 film (22.3%), sementara rating terkecil adalah G dengan 178 film (17.8%). Distribusi antar rating tergolong cukup merata, menunjukkan bahwa toko menyediakan variasi konten yang menjangkau berbagai segmen penonton.

3.2 Panjang Film per Rating

2 
Panjang Film per Rating
Uji perbedaan durasi film antar kategori rating

3.2.1 Hipotesis

Hipotesis Uji Kruskal-Wallis

\[H_0: \text{Tidak terdapat perbedaan distribusi panjang film antar kelompok rating}\] \[H_1: \text{Terdapat perbedaan distribusi panjang film minimal pada satu kelompok rating}\] \[\alpha = 0.05\]

3.2.2 Statistika Deskriptif per Rating

film_length <- dbGetQuery(con, "
  SELECT rating, length FROM film WHERE length IS NOT NULL")

desc_length <- film_length %>%
  group_by(rating) %>%
  summarise(
    n            = n(),
    `Rata-rata`  = round(mean(length), 2),
    Median       = median(length),
    `Std. Dev`   = round(sd(length), 2),
    Min          = min(length),
    Max          = max(length),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
desc_length

3.2.3 Uji Kruskal-Wallis

kw_test <- kruskal.test(length ~ rating, data = film_length)
print(kw_test)
## 
##  Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data:  length by rating
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 8.8015, df = 4, p-value = 0.06626
Statistik Uji Kruskal-Wallis

\[H = \frac{12}{N(N+1)} \sum_{j=1}^{k} \frac{R_j^2}{n_j} - 3(N+1)\] di mana \(N\) = total observasi, \(k\) = jumlah kelompok, \(R_j\) = jumlah rank kelompok \(j\), \(n_j\) = ukuran kelompok \(j\)

## KESIMPULAN: p-value = 0.0663 >= 0.05 → Gagal Tolak H₀
##  Tidak ada perbedaan signifikan panjang film antar rating.

3.3 Top 10 Kategori Film

3 
Top 10 Kategori Film Terbanyak
Kategori genre dengan jumlah film terbanyak dalam katalog
kategori_data <- dbGetQuery(con, "
  SELECT c.name AS kategori, COUNT(fc.film_id) AS jumlah_film
  FROM category c
  JOIN film_category fc ON c.category_id = fc.category_id
  GROUP BY c.name ORDER BY jumlah_film DESC LIMIT 10")
kategori_data
ggplot(kategori_data, aes(x = reorder(kategori, jumlah_film),
                          y = jumlah_film, fill = jumlah_film)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = jumlah_film), hjust = -0.3,
            size = 3.8, fontface = "bold", color = "#1a1a2e") +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#a8d5e2", high = "#0d1b2a") +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15))) +
  labs(title   = "Top 10 Kategori Film Terbanyak",
       subtitle = "Berdasarkan jumlah film per kategori genre",
       x = NULL, y = "Jumlah Film",
       caption = "Sumber: Database Sakila") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5,
                                     family = "serif", size = 14),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, color = "gray50"),
        panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "none")

📝 Interpretasi

Kategori Sports menduduki posisi teratas dengan 74 film, diikuti oleh Foreign dengan 73 film dan Family dengan 69 film. Dominasi kategori-kategori ini menunjukkan preferensi kurator toko dalam membangun koleksi yang berorientasi pada genre populer.

3.4 Pelanggan Aktif vs Tidak Aktif per Store

4 
Pelanggan Aktif vs Tidak Aktif per Store
Uji perbedaan proporsi keaktifan pelanggan antar toko

3.4.1 Hipotesis

Hipotesis Uji Chi-Square

\[H_0: \text{Tidak ada hubungan antara store dan status keaktifan pelanggan}\] \[H_1: \text{Terdapat hubungan antara store dan status keaktifan pelanggan}\] \[\alpha = 0.05\]

3.4.2 Data Pelanggan per Store

aktif_store <- dbGetQuery(con, "
  SELECT store_id,
         SUM(active = 1) AS aktif,
         SUM(active = 0) AS tidak_aktif,
         COUNT(*)        AS total
  FROM customer GROUP BY store_id")

aktif_store$pct_aktif <- round(aktif_store$aktif /
                               aktif_store$total * 100, 2)
aktif_store

3.4.3 Uji Chi-Square

tabel_chi <- matrix(c(aktif_store$aktif, aktif_store$tidak_aktif),
                    nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE)
chi_test  <- chisq.test(tabel_chi)
print(chi_test)
## 
##  Pearson's Chi-squared test with Yates' continuity correction
## 
## data:  tabel_chi
## X-squared = 6.8695e-29, df = 1, p-value = 1
Statistik Uji Chi-Square

\[\chi^2 = \sum_{i=1}^{r}\sum_{j=1}^{c} \frac{(O_{ij} - E_{ij})^2}{E_{ij}}\] di mana \(O_{ij}\) = frekuensi observasi, \(E_{ij}\) = frekuensi ekspektasi pada baris \(i\) kolom \(j\)

aktif_long <- aktif_store %>%
  select(store_id, aktif, tidak_aktif) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(aktif, tidak_aktif),
               names_to = "status", values_to = "jumlah") %>%
  mutate(status      = ifelse(status == "aktif", "Aktif", "Tidak Aktif"),
         store_label = paste("Store", store_id))

ggplot(aktif_long, aes(x = store_label, y = jumlah, fill = status)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge", width = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = jumlah),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.6),
            vjust = -0.5, size = 4, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Aktif"       = "#0f7b6c",
                               "Tidak Aktif" = "#c0392b")) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15))) +
  labs(title    = "Pelanggan Aktif vs Tidak Aktif per Store",
       subtitle = paste0("Uji Chi-Square: \u03c7\u00b2 = ",
                         round(chi_test$statistic, 4),
                         ", p-value = ", round(chi_test$p.value, 4)),
       x = NULL, y = "Jumlah Pelanggan", fill = "Status",
       caption = "Sumber: Database Sakila") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5,
                                     family = "serif", size = 14),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, color = "gray50"),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank())

📊 
Karena p-value >= 0.05, maka H₀ gagal ditolak, artinya tidak terdapat perbedaan signifikan proporsi keaktifan pelanggan antar toko.

3.5 Top 15 Negara Asal Pelanggan

5 
Top 15 Negara Asal Pelanggan
Distribusi geografis pelanggan berdasarkan negara
negara_data <- dbGetQuery(con, "
  SELECT co.country, COUNT(cu.customer_id) AS jumlah_pelanggan
  FROM customer cu
  JOIN address  a  ON cu.address_id = a.address_id
  JOIN city     ci ON a.city_id     = ci.city_id
  JOIN country  co ON ci.country_id = co.country_id
  GROUP BY co.country ORDER BY jumlah_pelanggan DESC LIMIT 15")
negara_data
ggplot(negara_data, aes(x = reorder(country, jumlah_pelanggan),
                        y = jumlah_pelanggan, fill = jumlah_pelanggan)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.72) +
  geom_text(aes(label = jumlah_pelanggan), hjust = -0.3,
            size = 3.5, fontface = "bold", color = "#1a1a2e") +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#f9ca74", high = "#c8390a") +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15))) +
  labs(title    = "Top 15 Negara Asal Pelanggan",
       subtitle = "Berdasarkan jumlah pelanggan terdaftar per negara",
       x = NULL, y = "Jumlah Pelanggan",
       caption = "Sumber: Database Sakila") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5,
                                     family = "serif", size = 14),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, color = "gray50"),
        panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "none")

📝 Interpretasi

Negara dengan pelanggan terbanyak adalah India dengan 60 pelanggan. Hal ini menunjukkan bahwa basis pelanggan tersebar secara global, mencerminkan jangkauan internasional dari sistem simulasi toko film ini.

3.6 Total Pendapatan per Store

6 
Total Pendapatan per Store
Perbandingan revenue antar toko dengan uji t independen

3.6.1 Hipotesis

Hipotesis Uji T Dua Sampel Independen

\[H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 \quad \text{(tidak ada perbedaan rata-rata nilai transaksi antar toko)}\] \[H_1: \mu_1 \neq \mu_2 \quad \text{(terdapat perbedaan rata-rata nilai transaksi antar toko)}\] \[\alpha = 0.05\]

3.6.2 Ringkasan Revenue per Store

revenue_store <- dbGetQuery(con, "
  SELECT s.store_id,
         COUNT(p.payment_id) AS total_transaksi,
         SUM(p.amount)       AS total_revenue,
         AVG(p.amount)       AS rata_rata,
         MIN(p.amount)       AS minimum,
         MAX(p.amount)       AS maksimum
  FROM payment p JOIN staff s ON p.staff_id = s.staff_id
  GROUP BY s.store_id")
revenue_store

3.6.3 Uji T-Test Independen

pay_store <- dbGetQuery(con, "
  SELECT s.store_id, p.amount
  FROM payment p JOIN staff s ON p.staff_id = s.staff_id")

t_test <- t.test(amount ~ store_id, data = pay_store)
print(t_test)
## 
##  Welch Two Sample t-test
## 
## data:  amount by store_id
## t = -2.374, df = 16041, p-value = 0.01761
## alternative hypothesis: true difference in means between group 1 and group 2 is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.16168574 -0.01544169
## sample estimates:
## mean in group 1 mean in group 2 
##        4.157251        4.245815
Statistik Uji T Welch

\[t = \frac{\bar{X}_1 - \bar{X}_2}{\sqrt{\dfrac{s_1^2}{n_1} + \dfrac{s_2^2}{n_2}}}\] di mana \(\bar{X}\) = rata-rata sampel, \(s^2\) = variansi sampel, \(n\) = ukuran sampel

revenue_store$store_label <- paste("Store", revenue_store$store_id)

ggplot(revenue_store, aes(x = store_label, y = total_revenue,
                          fill = store_label)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0("$", formatC(total_revenue,
                format = "f", digits = 2, big.mark = ","))),
            vjust = -0.5, size = 4.2, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Store 1" = "#0d1b2a",
                               "Store 2" = "#c8922a")) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = dollar_format(),
                     expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15))) +
  labs(title    = "Total Pendapatan per Store",
       subtitle = paste0("Uji T-Test Welch: t = ",
                         round(t_test$statistic, 4),
                         ", p-value = ", round(t_test$p.value, 4)),
       x = NULL, y = "Total Revenue (USD)",
       caption = "Sumber: Database Sakila") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5,
                                     family = "serif", size = 14),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, color = "gray50"),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "none")

📊 
Karena p-value < 0.05, maka H₀ ditolak, artinya terdapat perbedaan signifikan rata-rata nilai transaksi antara Store 1 dan Store 2.

3.7 Trend Pendapatan Bulanan

7 
Trend Pendapatan Bulanan
Pola revenue dari waktu ke waktu berdasarkan data payment
trend_bulanan <- dbGetQuery(con, "
  SELECT DATE_FORMAT(payment_date, '%Y-%m') AS bulan,
         COUNT(*)    AS total_transaksi,
         SUM(amount) AS total_revenue,
         AVG(amount) AS rata_rata
  FROM payment GROUP BY bulan ORDER BY bulan")

trend_bulanan$bulan <- as.Date(paste0(trend_bulanan$bulan, "-01"))
trend_bulanan
📅 
Pendapatan tertinggi terjadi pada July 2005 senilai $28,368.91 dengan 6709 transaksi.
Pendapatan terendah terjadi pada February 2006 senilai $514.18.
ggplot(trend_bulanan, aes(x = bulan, y = total_revenue)) +
  geom_area(fill = "#a8d8ea", alpha = 0.45) +
  geom_line(color = "#0d1b2a", linewidth = 1.4) +
  geom_point(color = "#c8922a", size = 3.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0("$", round(total_revenue, 0))),
            vjust = -0.9, size = 3, color = "#333344") +
  scale_x_date(date_labels = "%b %Y", date_breaks = "1 month") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels  = dollar_format(),
                     expand  = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.2))) +
  labs(title    = "Trend Pendapatan Bulanan",
       subtitle = "Total revenue per bulan dari seluruh transaksi pembayaran",
       x = "Bulan", y = "Total Pendapatan (USD)",
       caption = "Sumber: Database Sakila") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5,
                                     family = "serif", size = 14),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, color = "gray50"),
        axis.text.x   = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

3.8 Pendapatan per Kategori Film

8 
Pendapatan per Kategori Film
Kontribusi revenue dari setiap genre film
rev_kategori <- dbGetQuery(con, "
  SELECT c.name AS kategori,
         COUNT(p.payment_id) AS total_transaksi,
         SUM(p.amount)       AS total_revenue,
         AVG(p.amount)       AS rata_rata
  FROM payment       p
  JOIN rental        r  ON p.rental_id    = r.rental_id
  JOIN inventory     i  ON r.inventory_id = i.inventory_id
  JOIN film_category fc ON i.film_id      = fc.film_id
  JOIN category      c  ON fc.category_id = c.category_id
  GROUP BY c.name ORDER BY total_revenue DESC")
rev_kategori
ggplot(rev_kategori, aes(x = reorder(kategori, total_revenue),
                         y = total_revenue, fill = total_revenue)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.72) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0("$", round(total_revenue, 0))),
            hjust = -0.12, size = 3.3, fontface = "bold",
            color = "#1a1a2e") +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#b8e8d8", high = "#0d4a3a") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = dollar_format(),
                     expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.18))) +
  labs(title    = "Total Pendapatan per Kategori Film",
       subtitle = "Kategori genre mana yang paling menguntungkan?",
       x = NULL, y = "Total Pendapatan (USD)",
       caption = "Sumber: Database Sakila") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5,
                                     family = "serif", size = 14),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, color = "gray50"),
        panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "none")

📝 Interpretasi

Kategori Sports menghasilkan revenue tertinggi senilai $5,314.21 dengan 1179 transaksi. Di sisi lain, kategori Music menghasilkan revenue paling rendah senilai $3,417.72. Perbedaan ini dapat menjadi acuan bagi manajemen dalam mengoptimalkan komposisi koleksi film.

3.9 Nilai Transaksi per Staf

9 
Nilai Transaksi per Staf
Uji perbedaan rata-rata nilai transaksi antar staf

3.9.1 Hipotesis

Hipotesis Uji T Dua Sampel Independen

\[H_0: \mu_{\text{staf}_1} = \mu_{\text{staf}_2} \quad \text{(tidak ada perbedaan rata-rata nilai transaksi antar staf)}\] \[H_1: \mu_{\text{staf}_1} \neq \mu_{\text{staf}_2} \quad \text{(terdapat perbedaan rata-rata nilai transaksi antar staf)}\] \[\alpha = 0.05\]

3.9.2 Statistika Deskriptif per Staf

transaksi_staf <- dbGetQuery(con, "
  SELECT CONCAT(s.first_name, ' ', s.last_name) AS nama_staf,
         s.store_id, p.amount
  FROM payment p JOIN staff s ON p.staff_id = s.staff_id")

desc_staf <- transaksi_staf %>%
  group_by(nama_staf, store_id) %>%
  summarise(
    `Jumlah Transaksi` = n(),
    `Rata-rata ($)`    = round(mean(amount), 4),
    `Median ($)`       = round(median(amount), 4),
    `Std. Dev ($)`     = round(sd(amount), 4),
    `Min ($)`          = min(amount),
    `Max ($)`          = max(amount),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
desc_staf

3.9.3 Uji T-Test Independen

staf_names <- unique(transaksi_staf$nama_staf)
grp1       <- transaksi_staf$amount[transaksi_staf$nama_staf == staf_names[1]]
grp2       <- transaksi_staf$amount[transaksi_staf$nama_staf == staf_names[2]]
t_staf     <- t.test(grp1, grp2)
print(t_staf)
## 
##  Welch Two Sample t-test
## 
## data:  grp1 and grp2
## t = -2.374, df = 16041, p-value = 0.01761
## alternative hypothesis: true difference in means is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.16168574 -0.01544169
## sample estimates:
## mean of x mean of y 
##  4.157251  4.245815
📊 
Karena p-value < 0.05, maka H₀ ditolak, artinya terdapat perbedaan signifikan rata-rata nilai transaksi antara kedua staf.

4 Kesimpulan

Berikut rangkuman hasil dari seluruh analisis yang telah dilakukan:

No Analisis Metode Kesimpulan
1 Distribusi Rating Film Deskriptif Rating PG-13 mendominasi katalog
2 Panjang Film per Rating Kruskal-Wallis Lihat hasil uji di atas
3 Top 10 Kategori Film Deskriptif Sports menjadi kategori terbanyak
4 Pelanggan Aktif per Store Chi-Square Lihat hasil uji di atas
5 Distribusi Negara Pelanggan Deskriptif Pelanggan tersebar secara global
6 Revenue per Store T-Test Lihat hasil uji di atas
7 Trend Pendapatan Bulanan Time Series Revenue bervariasi per bulan
8 Revenue per Kategori Deskriptif Sports menghasilkan revenue tertinggi
9 Transaksi per Staf T-Test Lihat hasil uji di atas
